title: Real time synchronisation of a free-running atomic clock time base with UTC using GNSS signals for application in experimental physics
abstract: We present the results obtained by applying, in real-time, a correction method to precisely synchronize a time base generated from a free-running atomic clock with the Coordinated Universal Time (UTC). The method uses the Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) signals to have regular time comparisons between the atomic clock generated time base and the GPS Time, perform linear fits of the measurements and extrapolate a correction to apply to the free-running signal. In this work, we apply for the first time this method in real-time. Two atomic clocks were tested, a low-cost Rubidium clock and a more expensive magnetic Caesium clock. We demonstrate that we can obtain a residual difference between the clock time base and the French official realization of UTC (UTC(OP)) in the range of $\pm 15$ ns with no apparent residual drift.
